title: quercus pubescens subsp. palensis
intro: Quercus pubescens subsp. palensis, also known as Japanese evergreen oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus pubescens subsp. palensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Japanese evergreen oak are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
